text:
There was once a bird who lived in the darkest part of an enchanted forest.

Each day, this bird fluttered around its damp, shadowy home searching for crumbs left behind by forest creatures. It never felt like it should have such an dreary existence. Somehow, there was always something fluttering just out of reach, or a glimmer that never became a feast. The birds in the forest whispered tales of far-off lands brimming with colors, songs, and tastes.

One cloudy afternoon, the bird decided enough was enough. It didn’t care if it became dinner tonight—it wasn’t going to let this continue. It took flight to visit the wise old owl who nested high atop the oldest oak tree.

After a long journey, the bird reached its destination and perched nervously on the owl’s branch. “What brings you here?” asked the owl, with great curiosity etched across his feathers.

“The wind sings different tunes today,” the bird began. “I yearn for more
